item:
Concept: “break.interrupt (X breaks Y — Y is a continuing happening and because of what X does it stops; as in "break a streak" / "break the cycle", NOT as in "break a glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.interrupt” as described above, X — what breaks the continuing thing — is a something: a person, a thing, or a happening; it need not be a person.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.interrupt

item:
Concept: “break.interrupt (X breaks Y — Y is a continuing happening and because of what X does it stops; as in "break a streak" / "break the cycle", NOT as in "break a glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.interrupt” as described above, Y — what is broken — is a something: a continuing happening (a silence, a streak, a cycle); not a person, and not a material object one can hold.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.interrupt

item:
Concept: “break.interrupt (X breaks Y — Y is a continuing happening and because of what X does it stops; as in "break a streak" / "break the cycle", NOT as in "break a glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.interrupt” as described above, the breaking takes place at some time — there is a time at which the continuing thing stops. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.interrupt

item:
Concept: “break.malfunction (X breaks Y — Y stops being able to do what it is for, though it stays in one piece; as in "break the alarm clock", NOT as in "break a glass" or "break a promise")”
Statement: In every case of “break.malfunction” as described above, X — the one who breaks Y — is a someone: a person, or people. (On this reading, if a power surge rather than anyone's doing stops the clock, this concept does not apply.) Normally X did something to Y.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.malfunction

item:
Concept: “break.malfunction (X breaks Y — Y stops being able to do what it is for, though it stays in one piece; as in "break the alarm clock", NOT as in "break a glass" or "break a promise")”
Statement: In every case of “break.malfunction” as described above, Y — what is broken — is a something: a thing, like a clock, a machine, or a tool; not a person and not a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.malfunction

item:
Concept: “break.malfunction (X breaks Y — Y stops being able to do what it is for, though it stays in one piece; as in "break the alarm clock", NOT as in "break a glass" or "break a promise")”
Statement: In every case of “break.malfunction” as described above, the breaking takes place at some time — there is a time after which Y no longer works as it should. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.malfunction

item:
Concept: “break.shatter (X breaks Y — X does something to Y and Y comes apart into many small pieces; as in "break a glass", NOT as in "break a promise" or "break the silence")”
Statement: In every case of “break.shatter” as described above, X — what does the breaking — is a something: anything whose doing can make Y come apart. It need not be a person: a storm or a falling rock can break a window. Normally it is a person or a moving thing.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.shatter

item:
Concept: “break.shatter (X breaks Y — X does something to Y and Y comes apart into many small pieces; as in "break a glass", NOT as in "break a promise" or "break the silence")”
Statement: In every case of “break.shatter” as described above, Y — what gets broken — is a something: a thing, not a time and not a place; the thing that comes apart into pieces.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.shatter

item:
Concept: “break.shatter (X breaks Y — X does something to Y and Y comes apart into many small pieces; as in "break a glass", NOT as in "break a promise" or "break the silence")”
Statement: In every case of “break.shatter” as described above, the breaking takes place at some time — there is a time such that before it Y was one thing and after it Y is in pieces. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.shatter

item:
Concept: “break.shatter-become (Y breaks — Y comes apart into many small pieces, with no breaker said; as in "the figurine broke", NOT as in "he broke the glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.shatter-become” as described above, Y — the thing that breaks — is a something: a thing, not a time and not a place; the thing that comes apart into pieces.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.shatter-become

item:
Concept: “break.shatter-become (Y breaks — Y comes apart into many small pieces, with no breaker said; as in "the figurine broke", NOT as in "he broke the glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.shatter-become” as described above, the breaking takes place at some time — there is a time such that before it Y was one thing and after it Y is in pieces. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.shatter-become

item:
Concept: “break.violate (X breaks Y — Y is words such as a promise, a law, or a rule, and X does not do what these words say; as in "break a promise" / "break the law", NOT as in "break a glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.violate” as described above, X — the one who breaks the promise, the law, or the rule — is a someone: a person, or people. A storm cannot break a promise.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.violate

item:
Concept: “break.violate (X breaks Y — Y is words such as a promise, a law, or a rule, and X does not do what these words say; as in "break a promise" / "break the law", NOT as in "break a glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.violate” as described above, Y — what is broken — is a something: the words (a promise, a law, a rule); not a person and not a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.violate

item:
Concept: “break.violate (X breaks Y — Y is words such as a promise, a law, or a rule, and X does not do what these words say; as in "break a promise" / "break the law", NOT as in "break a glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.violate” as described above, the thing done — what the words say cannot be done — is a something: a doable something; not a person, not a time, not a place.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.violate

item:
Concept: “break.violate (X breaks Y — Y is words such as a promise, a law, or a rule, and X does not do what these words say; as in "break a promise" / "break the law", NOT as in "break a glass")”
Statement: In every case of “break.violate” as described above, the breaking takes place at some time — there is a time at which X does what the words say cannot be done. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:break.violate

item:
Concept: “find.discover (X finds Y — X comes to know something after not knowing it; as in "find a flaw" / "she found that he had lied", NOT as in "find the keys")”
Statement: In every case of “find.discover” as described above, X — the one who finds out — is a someone: a person, or people.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:find.discover

item:
Concept: “find.discover (X finds Y — X comes to know something after not knowing it; as in "find a flaw" / "she found that he had lied", NOT as in "find the keys")”
Statement: In every case of “find.discover” as described above, Y — what is found out — is a something: a flaw, an answer, a fact; not a time and not a place.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:find.discover

item:
Concept: “find.discover (X finds Y — X comes to know something after not knowing it; as in "find a flaw" / "she found that he had lied", NOT as in "find the keys")”
Statement: In every case of “find.discover” as described above, the finding-out takes place at some time — there is a time at which X comes to know Y. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:find.discover

item:
Concept: “find.locate (X finds Y — X comes to know where Y is after not knowing; as in "find the keys", NOT as in "find a flaw" or "find that ...")”
Statement: In every case of “find.locate” as described above, X — the finder — is a someone: a person, or another being that can see and come to know. (On this reading a metal detector does not itself ‘find’ — the someone using it does.)
subject: urn:kernel-v1:find.locate

item:
Concept: “find.locate (X finds Y — X comes to know where Y is after not knowing; as in "find the keys", NOT as in "find a flaw" or "find that ...")”
Statement: In every case of “find.locate” as described above, Y — what is found — is a something: a thing that is somewhere and can be seen; not a time.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:find.locate

item:
Concept: “find.locate (X finds Y — X comes to know where Y is after not knowing; as in "find the keys", NOT as in "find a flaw" or "find that ...")”
Statement: In every case of “find.locate” as described above, there is a place — the place where Y is; the finding fixes it, and X comes to know it.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:find.locate

item:
Concept: “friend.figurative (X is a friend of Y — X is not a person, but Y thinks about X the way one thinks about a friend; as in "imaginary friend" / a beloved toy or dog spoken of as a friend)”
Statement: In every case of “friend.figurative” as described above, X — the friend — is a something: it need not be a person; an imaginary friend, a beloved toy, or an animal companion can fill it.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:friend.figurative

item:
Concept: “friend.figurative (X is a friend of Y — X is not a person, but Y thinks about X the way one thinks about a friend; as in "imaginary friend" / a beloved toy or dog spoken of as a friend)”
Statement: In every case of “friend.figurative” as described above, Y — the one who has the friend — is a someone: a person, or people.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:friend.figurative

item:
Concept: “friend.person (X is a friend of Y — X and Y are people who know each other well with affection and trust; as in "my best friend", NOT as in "imaginary friend" or a toy spoken of as a friend)”
Statement: In every case of “friend.person” as described above, X — the friend — is a someone: a person. On this reading a toy or an app is not a friend — that way of speaking belongs to the figurative sense.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:friend.person

item:
Concept: “friend.person (X is a friend of Y — X and Y are people who know each other well with affection and trust; as in "my best friend", NOT as in "imaginary friend" or a toy spoken of as a friend)”
Statement: In every case of “friend.person” as described above, Y — the one whose friend X is — is a someone: a person.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:friend.person

item:
Concept: “make.cause (X makes Y do something — because of what X does, Y does it; as in "the ads made me buy it" / "she made him apologize", NOT as in "make a chair")”
Statement: In every case of “make.cause” as described above, X — the one who makes Y do something — is a someone: a person, or people.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:make.cause

item:
Concept: “make.cause (X makes Y do something — because of what X does, Y does it; as in "the ads made me buy it" / "she made him apologize", NOT as in "make a chair")”
Statement: In every case of “make.cause” as described above, Y — the one made to do something — is a someone: a person, or people. A stone cannot be made to apologize.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:make.cause

item:
Concept: “make.cause (X makes Y do something — because of what X does, Y does it; as in "the ads made me buy it" / "she made him apologize", NOT as in "make a chair")”
Statement: In every case of “make.cause” as described above, the making-happen takes place at some time — there is a time at which X does what gets Y to act. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:make.cause

item:
Concept: “make.create (X makes Y — because of what X does, Y comes to exist; as in "make a chair" / "make a cake", NOT as in "make someone sad" or "make the bed" or "make the train")”
Statement: In every case of “make.create” as described above, X — the maker — is a someone: a person, or people. (On this reading, if no one made it, nothing was ‘made’.)
subject: urn:kernel-v1:make.create

item:
Concept: “make.create (X makes Y — because of what X does, Y comes to exist; as in "make a chair" / "make a cake", NOT as in "make someone sad" or "make the bed" or "make the train")”
Statement: In every case of “make.create” as described above, Y — what is made — is a something: a thing that comes to exist; not a time and not a place.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:make.create

item:
Concept: “make.create (X makes Y — because of what X does, Y comes to exist; as in "make a chair" / "make a cake", NOT as in "make someone sad" or "make the bed" or "make the train")”
Statement: In every case of “make.create” as described above, the making takes place at some time — there is a time before which Y was not and after which Y is. Normally one can say when.
subject: urn:kernel-v1:make.create
